column38:
1. Data systems - It’s important that models are developed for the data gaps that’s e currently face for better decision making
2. Institutional Approaches - Budgeting and allocation to each project should be based on conditions and not on whims and political emotions 
3. Technical Capacity - we need structured training in asset management especially among engineers involved in Mtce since most of them are not clear of asset management practices and its attendant requirements. Data analysis, AI and such technologies should firm partyof the knowledge training
column39:
1. Forecasting - the best means is to use actual data, since these datasets are not available, use of models can help
2. Modeling - Models are only as good as the data used to develop them. I’m n my view the data sets available may need some argumentation to set good models for use in decision making. But models help in the absence of good data bases. The assumptions used in their development needs to be solid
3. Planning, asset management and allocation decisions should be based on good data - this is the reason many projects are failing early. We don’t use good data and this is because we don’t have good data. Good data acquisition, analysis and model should be the standard practice.

Implement a Performance-Based System
The current system is designed for unit-rate contracts. Shifting to a Performance-Based Road Management System (PBRMS) would tie contractor payments to the actual "Level of Service" (LOS) delivered. This approach, which requires digital tools for monitoring performance indicators, incentivizes quality workmanship and long-term durability.
